text_en: Noting that Italy has been entrusted by the United Nations with the administration of the Trust Territory of Somalia and that it is currently exercising its responsibilities to the United Nations for the administration of the Trust Territory of Somalia in accordance with the provisions of Chapters XII and XIII of the Charter,

text_fr: Considcrant que  l'Italie  cloit  etre  mise  en  mesure d'exercer ces  responsabilites  avec  tme  entiere  efficacite,

text_fr: Estimant en consequence  necessaire  que l'Italie devienne membre du Conseil  <le  tutelle et qu'a cette fin elle  soit  admise  clans  !'Organisation des  Nations Cnies, et  considerant  en  outre  que  l'Italie remplit  Jes  condi› tions  que  le  paragraphe  premier  de  !'Article 4  de  la Charte exige  pour  cette  admission,
text_en: Considering, therefore, that Italy should become a member of the Council, and that for this purpose it should be accepted as a member of the Council, and considering, moreover, that Italy fulfils the conditions which Article 4(1) of the Charter requires for this admission,

text_fr: Reco111mande au  Conseil  de securite  de prenclre d'urgence  en  consideration  la  presente  resolution a l'effet de  recommander !'admission immediate cle  l'Ita!ie comme  Membre  des  ~ations  Unies.
text_en: This resolution has the effect of recommending to the Security Council the immediate admission of Ita!ie as a Member of the United Nations.

items_fr: 1. Le  Chapitre  XI  de  la  Charte des  Nations  Unies constitue  une  declaration  relative  aux.  Territoires  non autonomes.  Aux  termes de !'Article 73  e,  qui  fait  partie de ce  chapitre, les  Membres des  Nations  Unies  qui  ont ou  qui  assument  la  responsabilite  d'administrer  des Territoires  dont  Jes  populations  ne  s 'administrent pas encore  completement  elles-memes,  s'engagent  a :

« communiquer regulierement au Secretaire general. a titre d'information, sous reserve des  exigences  de  la securite  et  de  considerations  d 'ordre constitutionnel, des  renseignements  statistiques  et  autres  de  nature technique relatifs aux conditions economiques, sociales et  de  !'instruction dans  Jes  territoires  dont  ils  sont respectivement responsables, autres que ceux auxquels s'appliquent les  Chapitres  XII  et  XIII».

2.  Par  sa  resolution  142  (II)  du  3  novembre  1947, I' Assemblee  generale  a  approuve  l'etablissement d 'un « Schema » destine a servir de guide aux Etats Membres pour  la  preparation  des  renseignements  a transmettre en vertu de  !'Article 73  e.  Parsa resolution 218  (III) du 3 novembre  1948,  elle  a  developpe  la  precedente  reso› lution et  a  recommande en  particulier,  en  meme  temps qu'elle  invitait  Jes  Etats  Membres  a transmettre  les renseignements  Jes plus recents  dont  ils  disposent, que  ceux-ci  fassent  connaitre  Jes  modifications  inter› venues  dans  Jes  donnees  statistiques  et  tous  autres changements  notables,  y  compris  les  progres  realises dans  !'execution des  programmes  de  developpement; ces  modifications  se  referent  a l'annee precedente,  et concernent  les  problemes  vises  a l' Article  73  e  de  la Charte.  Elle  a  precise  qu'il  n'etait  pas  necessaire  de repeter  les  renseignements  fournis  anterieurement  si !'on prenait  soin  de  faire  reference  aux  sources  appro› priees.  Par cette meme  resolution elle  a encore invite le Secretaire  general  a preparer  des  resumes  et  analyses complets des  renseignements transmis en  1949,  stipulant que,  par  la  suite,  ces  documents  seraient  etablis  tous ies  trois  ans,  et  que,  dans  l'intervalle, des  documents annuels  complementaires  feraient  apparaitre  les  modi› fications  dans les  donnees  statistiques et  autres change› ments notables intervenus au cours de l 'annee precedente.

3.  En  1951  l'Assemblee generale  a  approuve;sur la recommandation  du  Comite  special  pour l'examen des renseignements  transmis  en  vertu  de  I' Article  73  e  de la  Charte, la  revision  du  Schema.
·  Par  la  resolution  551 (VI)  figurant  ci-dessus  l'Assemblec generate  a approm·e  le Schema  reYise  en  y  incorporant l'amcnclement  ( A/1998)  prescnte  a sa  Jj2eme  seance  pleniere
4. Le  present  document  contient le  textc  du  Schema revise.
items_en: 1. Chapter XI of the Charter of the United Nations constitutes a declaration relating to the Non-Self-Governing Territories. Article 73 e, which forms part of this chapter, provides that Members of the United Nations who have or assume responsibility for the administration of Territories whose populations do not yet fully administer themselves, undertake to:

"to communicate regularly to the Secretary General. for information, subject to the requirements of security and constitutional considerations, statistical and other information of a technical nature relating to economic, social and educational conditions in the territories for which they are respectively responsible, other than those to which Chapters XII and XIII apply".

2. By its Resolution 142 (II) of 3 November 1947, the General Assembly approved the establishment of a "Schema" intended to serve as a guide for Member States in the preparation of the information to be transmitted under Article 73 (III) of 3 November 1948, it has developed the previous resolution and has recommended in particular, at the same time as it invited Member States to transmit the more recent information available to them, that they make known the changes which have been made in statistical data and any other significant changes, including the progress made in the implementation of the development programmes; these amendments are referred to in the previous year, and concern the problems which are aimed at in Article 73 (e) of the Charter. It has stated that it is not necessary to retrace the information previously provided if it is taken care to refer to the complete data sources.
3. In 1951 the General Assembly approved, on the recommendation of the Special Committee for the review of information transmitted under Article 73 e of the Charter, the revision of the Scheme.
· By resolution 551 (VI) above, the Assembly adopted the Schema reyise by incorporating the amendment (A/1998) prescnted at its plenary session
4. This document contains the text of the revised Schema.

items_fr: 1. Les  points mentionnes dans le  Schema ont parfois trait a des  facteurs  permanents tels  que la  superficie  ou la  geographic  d'un Territoire  donne.  En  ce  cas  ii  n'y aurait  aucun  interet  a repeter  des  renseignements  deja fournis.  Ceci  vaut  egalement  pour  certaines  caracte-· ristiques  comme  les  ressources  naturelles  ou les  grands traits de  l'economie d'un territ~ire ; ii  serait cependapt utile  de  mentionner  ici  les  changements  constates,  par exemple,  apres des  prospections geologiques  ou !'intro› duction  de  nouvelles  cultures.

2.  II  est demande une seconde categoric de  renseigne› ments  relatifs  aux  programmes  gouvernementaux  a long  terme  et  a  !'organisation  administrative. On suggere  que,  dans  ce  cas,  les  renseignements  ne  soient fournis  que  tous  les  trois  ans  (par  exemple,  en  ce  qui concerne  Jes  renseignements  transmis  en  1952,  sur  la situation au cours de l'annee precedente, administrative ou  civile).  II  est  par  ailleurs  souhaitable  que,  dans  le cas  de  renseignements  concernant  la  politique  fonda› mentale  des  gouvernements,  !'on fasse  chaque  annee une  reference  particuliere  a  ceux-ci,  meme  si  aucun changement  n'est intervenu.

3. On constatera qu'une troisieme categoric de rensei› gnements  consiste  en  grande  partie a fournir les  statis› tiques  relatives a l'annee en  cours  d'etude; ceux-ci devront etre fournis de facon tres detaillee chaque annee.

4.  S'agissant des  statistiques  demandees  a propos de certaines questions,  on  trouvera en  annexe  des  modeles auxquels  ii  est  recommande  de  se  conformer.  On  a suivi cette methode afin  que les Territoires qui jugent la chose  possible  se  conforment  aux  modeles  suggeres, tandis qu'au contraire d'autres Territoires seront libres de  fournir  les  renseignements  en  s'accommodant des pratiques  ou  des  moyens  actuels.

5. Partout  oil  la  situation  des  Territoires  le  rendra possible,  les  statistiques devront etre classees de maniere a mettre  en  lumiere  les  conditions  particulieres  aux populations indigene et  non  indigene  et leur  participa› tion  a  la  chose  publique  (par  exemple :  personnel administratif,  repartition  des  terres,  possibilites  d 'ins› truction).  Ces  renseignements  sont  demandes  en  parti› culier dans Ies  cas ou, du fait  de la loi ou de la pratique administrative,  ii  existe  des  distinctions  raciales  ou religieuses.

6. Dans le cas ou, en  vertu d'une convention generale en  matiere  economique,  sociale  ou  d'enseignement, des  renseignements  soot  transmis  a  une  institution internationale  par  des  Etats  Membres,  parties  a une telle  convention,  et  des  renseignements  qui  correspon› dent  a  certains renseignements  demandes  dans  le Schema soot transmis regulierement a des organisations internationales  fonctionnant  sous  l'egide  des  Nations Unies conformement aux dispositions prises  a cet effet, la  transmission  d'une capie  de  ces  renseignements  au Secretaire  general  des Nations  Unies  satisferait  a !'obligation  que  renferme  I' Article  73  e, en  ce  qui concerne la  matiere traitee.  Chaque fois  que des  rensei› gnements appropries soot publies, ii  n'est pas necessaire que les gouvernements reproduisent ces renseignements ; ii  suffira  d'une reference  au chapitre et a la  page  de  la publication  en  question  (avec  communication  de  la publication  elle-meme,  si  necessaire).
items_en: 1. The points mentioned in the Schema sometimes relate to permanent factors such as the area or geographical location of a given Territory. In this case there would be no interest in reproducing information already provided. This also applies to certain characteristics such as natural resources or the main features of the economy of a land; ii it would be useful to mention here changes observed, for example, after geological prospecting or the introduction of new cultures.

It is suggested that, in this case, information should only be provided every three years (for example, with regard to the information transmitted in 1952 on the situation during the previous year, whether administrative or civil). It is also desirable that, in the case of information concerning the fundamental policy of governments, a particular reference should be made to them every year, even if no change has taken place.

3. It will be found that a third categoric of rensei› gments consists in a large part of supplying the statistics relating to the year under study; these will have to be supplied in a detailed manner each year.

4. With regard to the statistics requested on certain issues, a list of models recommended for compliance is attached. This approach has been followed to ensure that the Territories that consider it possible to comply with the suggested models, while other Territories will be free to provide information in accordance with current practices or means.

5. Everywhere the situation of the Territories will make it possible, statistics will have to be classified in such a way as to give light to the special conditions of the indigenous and non-indigenous populations and to participate in public affairs (e.g. administrative personnel, land distribution, possibilities of insti tutions). This information is requested in particular in cases where, by virtue of the law or administrative practice, racial or religious distinctions exist.

6. In the case where, under a general agreement in the economic, social or educational field, information soot transmitted to an international institution by Member States, parties to such a convention, and information corresponding to certain information requested in the Scheme soot transmitted regularly to international organizations operating under the egide of the United Nations in accordance with the provisions adopted for that purpose, the transmission of a capie of such information to the Secretary-General of the United Nations would satisfy the obligation contained in Article 73e, with regard to the subject matter dealt with.

items_en: 1. In order to enable the Special Committee to review progress made in the Territories in the areas covered by Article 73 of the Treaty, Member States shall invite to provide a description of the principles and practical measures which light up the general trends in the Territories concerned, such as:
(a) Progress in the economic, social and educational fields, including the participation of the inhabitants in any common discussion of problems relating to these areas;

(b) Participation of the Territories in regional or special commissions of the Economic and Social Council, bodies of specialized institutions and regional commissions or conferences, including research organizations;

(c) Recourse to technical assistance from the United Nations and specialized agencies, or from other international bodies, including the manner in which such assistance has been integrated into long-term programmes for the development of the Territories;
(d) Measures taken to adopt, amend or implement international agreements of particular importance to the Territories.
